Scenario.overview: Healthcare Staff Simple APT41 DUST targets healthcare organizations using spear phishing and drive-by compromise to gain initial access, followed by custom command and control protocols and PowerShell exploitation for execution, leading to automated exfiltration and denial-of-service attacks.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Target Artifact (Protocols), PowerShell Interpreter, Command, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Custom Command and Control Protocol, PowerShell Script, Command Execution (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2013-6282 (Suggested): The get_user and put_user API functions of the Linux kernel fail to validate the target address when being used on ARM v6k/v7 platforms.
stage.description: The attacker sends spear phishing emails to healthcare staff using compromised email accounts, exploiting the trust relationship between the sender and recipient.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2024-29059 (Suggested): Microsoft.NET Framework contains an information disclosure vulnerability that exposes the ObjRef URI to an attacker, ultimately enabling remote code execution.
stage.description: The attacker compromises public health websites with malware-laced ads or malicious code, infecting visitors' devices during a drive-by download.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.kev: CVE-2020-5849 (Suggested): Unraid contains an authentication bypass vulnerability that allows attackers to gain access to the administrative interface.
stage.description: The attacker establishes command and control servers to remotely access compromised systems, utilizing custom command and control protocols for communication.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-27860 (Suggested): A vulnerability in the web management interface of FatPipe WARP, IPVPN, and MPVPN software allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to upload a file to any location on the filesystem.
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attacker utilizes PowerShell to execute malicious scripts on compromised systems, taking advantage of the powerful scripting capabilities of PowerShell.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker steals sensitive patient data and medical records through automated exfiltration, compromising the confidentiality and integrity of the data.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts critical healthcare services and causes operational downtime through denial-of-service attacks, impacting the availability of essential services.

Scenario.summary: APT41 DUST conducts a series of attacks against healthcare organizations, starting with spear phishing and drive-by compromise to gain initial access, followed by custom command and control protocols and PowerShell exploitation for execution, culminating in automated exfiltration and denial-of-service attacks.
